subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of their culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures
indicator_description: Identify the processes involved in naming a child.
examplars:
1. Discuss the outdooring and the naming process of a child in their community. • E.g. outdooring/ushering the baby, invitation of ancestors, giving the baby a name, blessing, presentation of gifts (father, person child was named after, family, community), and so on. NB: Let learners watch a video on the traditional naming process and discuss it.
2. Identify the items used in the naming process and write them. E.g.: water, basket, bucket, beads, basin, mat, powder, lantern, clothes (baby and mother).
3. Explain the significance of the items used for the naming ceremony. NB: In some communities the outdooring and the naming process are two different things.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of their culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures
indicator_description: Discuss the significance of the processes in naming a child.
examplars:
1. Read a words on naming ceremony in their culture.
2. Discuss the significance of the processes involved in naming a child as raised in the passage. E.g. • Welcomes the child into the family and community. • The child gets an identity.
3. Discuss a libation text used during naming ceremony.
4. Write the three main parts of a libation text.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of their culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures
indicator_description: Compare naming ceremonies in their culture with other Ghanaian cultures.
examplars:
1. Search for information on naming ceremonies of other Ghanaian cultures.
2. Discuss the similarities and differences between your culture and other Ghanaian cultures. NB: Use a table to show the similarities and differences. • Names given to the children. • The time for the ceremony. • Items used for the ceremony. • Process of giving the name. • Who gives the name.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: NAMING SYSTEMS: DAY NAMES, ORDER OF BIRTH NAMES
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of day-born names and order of birth names and relate the names to the days and order of birth
indicator_description: State the names of the days of the week and relate their names to the days.
examplars:
1. State the names of the days of the week and important occasion (e.g. festival) months of the year in their language.
2. Identify and write the day-born names (male and female) in your ethnic community.
3. Identify the days and write the names related to them.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: NAMING SYSTEMS: DAY NAMES, ORDER OF BIRTH NAMES
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of day-born names and order of birth names and relate the names to the days and order of birth
indicator_description: Explain how order-of-birth names are given to children.
examplars: 1. Discuss and list names given according to order of birth (male and female) in their ethnic community. NB: Discuss multiple birth names.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: NAMING SYSTEMS: DAY NAMES, ORDER OF BIRTH NAMES
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of day-born names and order of birth names and relate the names to the days and order of birth
indicator_description: Examine the similarities and differences in names between their culture and other Ghanaian cultures.
examplars:
1. Search for information on naming systems of other Ghanaian cultures.
2. Identify the similarities and differences in the naming systems (day-born names, order of birth names) of your culture and other Ghanaian cultures.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the clan system among their people
indicator_description: Describe the clan system and state some clans in their ethnic community.
examplars:
1. Explain what a clan is.
2. State some clans you know. NB: Learners associate themselves with the clans stated. E.g.: • Akan: Aduana, Agona, etc. • Dangme: Dangmebiawɛ, Piɛɛdoku, etc.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the clan system among their people
indicator_description: Identify the features and significance of the clan system.
examplars:
1. State the features of the clan system. E.g.: • Common ancestor. • Common totem. • Common funeral place. • Common taboo. • Shared names.
2. Discuss the importance of the clan system among their ethnic group. E.g. • Unity • Support system (Social, economic and political) • Identity • Land ownership

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the clan system among their people
indicator_description: Compare the clan system in their culture with other Ghanaian cultures.
examplars:
1. Search for information on the clan system of other Ghanaian cultures.
2. Identify the similarities and differences in the clan systems of their culture and other cultures.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge in and an understanding of how chiefs and queenmothers are selected, enstooled/ enskinned and compare their duties and responsibilities
indicator_description: Discuss how chiefs and queenmothers are selected in their ethnic community.
examplars:
1. Read a passage on how chiefs and queenmothers are selected in their community.
2. Identify the traditional leaders mentioned in the passage. E.g. chiefs,  chiefs, and so on.
3. Relate how chiefs and queenmothers are selected among their people to the passage read.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge in and an understanding of how chiefs and queenmothers are selected, enstooled/ enskinned and compare their duties and responsibilities
indicator_description: Discuss the processes involved in enstooling/enskinning chiefs and queenmothers.
examplars:
1. Discuss the qualities of a person who qualifies to be a chief or a queenmother among your people.
2. Create a profile of the qualities of a chief or queenmother.
3. Identify those who select the chief and queenmother among your people.
4. Role play the enstoolment/enskinment of chiefs and queenmothers. Learners watch a video on the topic before the role play. NB: Some cultures of Ghana do not have queenmothers but have female chiefs. The facilitator should make sure that ALL learners take active part in the role play.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge in and an understanding of how chiefs and queenmothers are selected, enstooled/ enskinned and compare their duties and responsibilities
indicator_description: Explain the functions of chiefs and queenmothers.
examplars:
1. Read a passage on the duties and responsibilities of chiefs and queenmothers.
2. List the functions of chiefs and queenmothers in the community as raised in the passage. E.g.: • Settling disputes. • Performance of traditional rites. • Bringing development. • Custodians of lands.
3. Write on the significance of the chieftaincy institution in their language.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate use of appropriate language in everyday communication (Conversation)
indicator_description: Explain and use appropriate words and phrases for various social situations.
examplars:
1. Read a passage on everyday communication.
2. Discuss the words and phrases for various social situations as raised in the passage. E.g. greetings and its responses, apologising, request, command, and so on.
3. Identify and use appropriate words and phrases for specific social situations (e.g. at the clinic/hospital, market, restaurant, football park, filling station, police station).

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate use of appropriate language in everyday communication (Conversation)
indicator_description: Describe the features and use language for everyday communication.
examplars:
1. Discuss the features of everyday communication. E.g. informal and simple words, brief phrases, slang, and so on.
2. Role play scenarios using everyday language (e.g. asking for directions, ordering food, reporting a crime).

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to listen to extended oral texts and answer questions on them
indicator_description: Answer literal, inferential and evaluative questions on a variety of oral texts.
examplars:
1. Listen to a variety of oral texts (e.g. stories, news, announcements) and answer questions on them.
2. Discuss the main idea and supporting details of the text.
3. Evaluate the speaker’s purpose and point of view.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to listen to extended oral texts and answer questions on them
indicator_description: Summarise oral texts in their language.
examplars:
1. Discuss the key points of an oral text.
2. Summarise the text using appropriate vocabulary and grammar in the language.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: SPEECH SOUNDS — VOWELS, CONSONANTS AND SYLLABLES
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the speech sounds of the language
indicator_description: Identify and classify vowels and consonants in the language.
examplars:
1. Produce the vowels and consonants of the language correctly.
2. Categorise vowels into oral and nasal, and short and long where applicable.
3. Classify consonants based on their place and manner of articulation.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: SPEECH SOUNDS — VOWELS, CONSONANTS AND SYLLABLES
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the speech sounds of the language
indicator_description: Examine the structure of syllables in the language.
examplars:
1. Explain what a syllable is.
2. Identify the components of a syllable (onset, peak, coda) where applicable.
3. Divide words into syllables and identify syllable types.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.2.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the tone system of the language
indicator_description: Identify and use tones to differentiate meaning in words.
examplars:
1. Identify the basic tones (high, low, falling, rising) of the language.
2. Produce words with different tones to show differences in meaning (e.g. minimal pairs).
3. Mark tones on words accurately where required.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.2.6.1 Demonstrate the ability to present information on everyday experiences
indicator_description: Present information on personal and community experiences.
examplars:
1. Plan a presentation on a familiar topic (e.g. my first day in school, a local festival).
2. Use appropriate non-verbal cues (e.g. eye contact, gestures) during presentation.
3. Deliver the presentation clearly and audibly in the language.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to read and understand a variety of texts
indicator_description: Read fluently and answer questions on various texts.
examplars:
1. Read aloud with correct pronunciation, stress, and intonation.
2. Use context to identify meanings of difficult words and phrases.
3. Answer literal, inferential, and evaluative questions on the text.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to read and understand a variety of texts
indicator_description: Summarise a read text in the language.
examplars:
1. Identify the main theme and themes of a text.
2. Summarise the text in their own words, maintaining the original meaning.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of the various sentence types
indicator_description: Identify and construct simple, compound and complex sentences.
examplars:
1. Explain the features of simple, compound, and complex sentences.
2. Use appropriate conjunctions to form compound and complex sentences.
3. Identify sentence types in a given text and construct your own.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (NOUNS, PRONOUNS AND ADJECTIVES)
content_standard: B7.4.2.1 Demonstrate the use of nouns, pronouns and adjectives in writing
indicator_description: Use various types of nouns and pronouns correctly in writing.
examplars:
1. Identify and use different types of nouns (e.g. proper, common, abstract) and pronouns (e.g. personal, possessive, relative) in sentences.
2. Demonstrate correct noun-pronoun agreement in writing.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (NOUNS, PRONOUNS AND ADJECTIVES)
content_standard: B7.4.2.1 Demonstrate the use of nouns, pronouns and adjectives in writing
indicator_description: Use adjectives appropriately in sentences.
examplars:
1. Identify and use various types of adjectives (e.g. quality, quantity, demonstrative) in writing.
2. Place adjectives in the correct order in a sentence.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (VERBS, ADVERBS, CONJUNCTIONS, POSTPOSITIONS/PREPOSITIONS)
content_standard: B7.4.3.1 Demonstrate the use of verbs, adverbs, conjunctions and postpositions/prepositions in writing
indicator_description: Use various types of verbs and tenses correctly in writing.
examplars:
1. Identify and use transitive, intransitive, and auxiliary verbs in sentences.
2. Demonstrate the correct use of tenses (e.g. past, present, future) and aspects (e.g. perfect, progressive) in writing.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: INTEGRATING GRAMMAR IN WRITTEN LANGUAGE (VERBS, ADVERBS, CONJUNCTIONS, POSTPOSITIONS/PREPOSITIONS)
content_standard: B7.4.3.1 Demonstrate the use of verbs, adverbs, conjunctions and postpositions/prepositions in writing
indicator_description: Use adverbs, conjunctions and postpositions/prepositions correctly in sentences.
examplars:
1. Identify and use various types of adverbs (e.g. time, place, manner) to modify verbs.
2. Use conjunctions and postpositions/prepositions appropriately to show relationships between words and phrases.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.4.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge of vocabulary, spelling and punctuation
indicator_description: Apply spelling rules and punctuation marks correctly in writing.
examplars:
1. Use correct spelling of words, including those with prefixes and suffixes.
2. Apply punctuation marks (e.g. full stop, comma, question mark, apostrophe) correctly to enhance clarity in writing.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: STRUCTURE AND ORGANISE IDEAS IN COMPOSITION WRITING
content_standard: B7.5.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to write coherent and well-organised compositions
indicator_description: Write various types of compositions using appropriate structure and style.
examplars:
1. Plan a composition using brainstorming or mind mapping.
2. Write a descriptive, narrative or argumentative composition with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion.
3. Use transitional devices to ensure coherence and flow in writing.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.6.1.1 Demonstrate appreciation of various genres of literature
indicator_description: Analyse the features and themes of folktales and songs.
examplars:
1. Identify the features of folktales (e.g. characters, setting, moral lesson).
2. Discuss the themes and social significance of traditional songs.
3. Summarise the plot and moral of a folktale in their own words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B7.6.1.1 Demonstrate appreciation of various genres of literature
indicator_description: Appreciate the elements and themes of prose, drama and poetry.
examplars:
1. Identify elements of prose (e.g. characterisation, plot), drama (e.g. dialogue, stage directions), and poetry (e.g. stanza, rhythm, rhyme).
2. Discuss themes and messages in selected literary works.
3. Write a critical appreciation or a short creative piece based on a genre.
